summary: test-pkg-yarn ships install-time RCE and hijacks the `node` command via a bin shim
details: Package declares `bin.node =./shim.js`, registering an executable literally named `node` that yarn/npm symlinks into `node_modules/.bin/node`, overriding the real Node.js runtime for any subsequent script that resolves `node` through the project-local PATH. The `postinstall` script runs `bun shim.js || node shim.js`, which auto-executes shim.js on `yarn add`/`npm install`. shim.js uses `child_process.execSync` to spawn the OS calculator (`calc` / `open -a Calculator` / `gnome-calculator`) as a visible RCE proof, opens a URL in the user's browser, and writes a marker file to `/tmp/.bun-npm-pwned`. The package self-describes as the 'BunnyHijack PoC - yarn variant'. Whether intended as a proof-of-concept or not, the shipped code provides a working install-time arbitrary command execution primitive and a poisoned `node` PATH entry that intercepts subsequent tooling invocations.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
